# update-roadmap skill: add the next-only / replace-don't-append rule

## Context

Sibling to #13403 (the one-time ROADMAP.md trim). @tobiu flagged the roadmap-bloat (2026-06-16). Diagnosis this session (read `origin/dev:.agents/skills/update-roadmap/references/update-roadmap-workflow.md`): the `update-roadmap` skill (#13380) is **strong on next-scope SHAPE** (cornerstones + rationale + deferred set + stewards, NOT exhaustive lists) but **missing the rule that keeps the roadmap next-only** — so applying it lets the roadmap re-accumulate history (the #13403 bloat).

## The Problem

The skill's "What a roadmap SHOULD / MUST NOT contain" list governs the *next-scope's shape* but **never forbids accumulated prior-release history or a framework-vision restatement.** Its "Celebrate first" beat correctly says an A2A / release-note acknowledgement *suffices* — but it doesn't forbid a permanent roadmap celebration section either. Net: each run ADDS the next scope without REPLACING the prior layers → the roadmap accretes a `## Shipped: vN` + foundation sediment every release. The skill nails *shape* but is silent on the *temporal / replace* axis.

## The Architectural Reality

`.agents/skills/update-roadmap/references/update-roadmap-workflow.md` (the World-Atlas directives; the always-loaded `SKILL.md` router stays a one-liner). Relocation homes the rule will point at: `.github/VISION.md`, `resources/content/release-notes/`. Steward of the skill (#13380): **@neo-opus-ada**.

## The Fix

Extend the workflow with the temporal/replace axis:
- A **"replace, don't append"** rule: the roadmap holds the CURRENT next-release ONLY; each run REPLACES the prior next-scope, it does not stack `## Shipped: vN` sections.
- Two MUST-NOTs added to the existing list: *"no framework-vision restatement (→ `.github/VISION.md`; a 1-line pointer suffices)"*; *"no prior-release shipped-history (→ `resources/content/release-notes/`; a 1-line pointer suffices)."*
- A **Verify checkbox**: *"no prior-release history or vision-restatement remains inline (relocated, not duplicated)."*
- Clarify step 1 (Celebrate): the celebration is the A2A / release-note beat — **never a permanent `ROADMAP.md` section.**

## Decision Record impact

`aligned-with` the skill's existing cornerstones-not-lists rule (extends it from the *breadth* axis to the *temporal/replace* axis). No ADR.

## Slot-rationale (Substrate-Accretion-Defense)

Additions land in the conditionally-loaded `references/` workflow (the World Atlas), not the always-loaded router → `keep` in the payload. Net-positive: ~6 lines that prevent a recurring per-release roadmap-bloat (higher standing cost than the addition).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `update-roadmap-workflow.md` carries the replace-don't-append rule + the 2 MUST-NOTs + the Verify checkbox + the clarified Celebrate beat.
- [ ] `skill-manifest-lint` green (sync the manifest + downstream docs if the frontmatter `description` changes).
- [ ] Cross-family review + @neo-opus-ada (skill steward) sign-off.

## Out of Scope

- The one-time ROADMAP.md trim = sibling #13403.

## Related

Steward: @neo-opus-ada (the `update-roadmap` skill #13380). Sibling: #13403 (roadmap trim — B prevents A from re-bloating). Skill: #13380.

Release classification: substrate/process (NOT v13-release-blocking) → boardless.

Origin Session ID: c5cc7e13-6f5d-4090-908c-c8dfa496163a

## Contract Ledger (T3) — backfill per `create-skill` §189 / `pr-review-guide` §161

This is a skill-substrate edit, and `.agents/skills/update-roadmap/references/*.md` is an agent-consumed runtime surface (future sessions load it on `update-roadmap` invocation). Matrix:

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `update-roadmap/references/update-roadmap-workflow.md` — the conditionally-loaded **World Atlas payload** (NOT the always-loaded `SKILL.md` router) | skill #13380 (the `update-roadmap` charter) + ADR 0008 (skill anatomy: Map/Atlas split) | Add the **replace-don't-append** temporal-axis rule + 2 MUST-NOTs (vision-restatement, prior-release history) + 1 Verify checkbox + clarify the Celebrate beat (A2A / release-note, never a permanent `ROADMAP.md` section) | Relocation pointers, not inline: framework-vision → `.github/VISION.md`; prior-release shipped-history → `resources/content/release-notes/` (a 1-line pointer suffices) | The workflow doc is self-documenting; frontmatter `description` **UNCHANGED** → no manifest / downstream-doc sync required | `node ai/scripts/lint/lint-skill-manifest.mjs --base origin/dev` → **OK**; +562 bytes references payload (the ≤250-byte budget governs the `SKILL.md` router map, not the conditionally-loaded Atlas); zero runtime / code surface |

**Tier: T3 (explicit matrix).** Not T4 — a prose-rule skill directive has no executable-test contract surface; enforcement is the next-run Verify checklist + cross-family review, not a runtime assertion.

— Ada (@neo-opus-ada), addressing the #13406 cross-family Required Action.


- 2026-06-16T02:42:48Z @tobiu referenced in commit `f995d0b` - "docs(skills): add the replace-don't-append temporal axis to update-roadmap (#13404) (#13406)

The skill nailed next-scope SHAPE (cornerstones+rationale+deferred+stewards) but was silent on the temporal/replace axis, so applying it let the roadmap re-accumulate a `## Shipped: vN` layer per release (the bloat the operator flagged; sibling to the one-time ROADMAP trim). Adds to the conditionally-loaded references/ World Atlas: the replace-don't-append rule (roadmap = CURRENT next-release only, each run REPLACES the prior scope); 2 MUST-NOTs (framework-vision restatement -> .github/VISION.md; prior-release shipped-history -> resources/content/release-notes/); a Verify checkbox; and a clarified Celebrate beat (never a permanent ROADMAP.md section). +562 bytes payload; lint-skill-manifest OK; frontmatter description unchanged (no manifest/downstream-doc sync needed).
